title: The Consumer Price Index (CPI)
description: Practice what you've learned about how to calculate the consumer price index and the rate of inflation, as well as the limitations of the CPI in this exercise.
topic_path: Economic indicators and the business cycle

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the consumer price index (CPI)?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: The change in prices that a consumer would have to pay for a fixed set of goods as a percentage of the same goods in a base year
Real GDP produced in one year divided by the real value of that GDP in another year
The cost inputs purchased by firms in a year divided by the price of the same inputs in a base year expressed as a percentage
The cost of labor purchased by firms in a year divided by the cost of the same amount of labor in a base year expressed as a percentage
The dollars a person needs in order to buy a fixed collection of goods in a year, compared to a base year

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what is necessary for consumers to maintain the same standard of living over time if there is inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Consumer incomes must increase at at least the same rate as the official rate of inflation
Consumer incomes must increase at a faster rate than the official rate of inflation
Government spending must increase at the same rate as the rate of inflation
Nominal incomes are the same over time
Business investment must increase at a faster rate than the official rate of inflation

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best explains why the consumer price index (CPI) may not accurately measure changes in the cost of living?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It does not account for changes in consumers' incomes
When prices of some goods go up, consumers buy less of those and more of goods that are cheaper
It doesn't include all prices, such as input costs to firms
When real GDP increases, the CPI doesn't change
It only includes goods purchased every day

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows the consumer price index (CPI) over several years for Bloominonionland.

Year | CPI
- | - 
$2015$ | $94$
$2016$ | $100$
$2017$ | $104$
$2018$ | $103$

**Which of the following conclusions can be drawn based on this data?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Prices increased over all four years
Prices increased between 2015 and 2017, but decreased between 2017 and 2018
2015 is the base year
The bundle of goods changed each year
real GDP increased every year except between 2017 and 2018

assessment_items.question_content:
The table below shows quantities purchased and the prices of the only two goods citizens of Bloominonionland buy over three years.

Year | Quantity of bubble tea | Price of bubble tea | Quantity of kangaroo steaks | Price of kangaroo steaks
- | - | - | - | - 
$2016$ | $10$ | $\$4$ | $5$ | $\$8$
$2017$ | $10$ | $\$6$ | $5$ | $\$12$
$2018$ | $10$ | $\$5$ | $5$ | $\$10$

**What is the consumer price index for 2017 using 2016 as a base year?** 

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is correct about the basket of goods that makes up a country's consumer price index (CPI)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It updates the prices of the goods in the basket annually, but keeps the goods constant
It updates the goods in the basket annually, but keeps prices constant
It updates both the prices of the goods and the goods themselves annually
It holds both the prices of goods and the goods themselves constant year after year
It consists of the same goods for every country that uses it

assessment_items.question_content:
The country of Ambrosia calculates inflation using a consumer price index (CPI). Between 2016 and 2017 the CPI increased from $200$ to $220$. 

**What was the inflation rate between 2016 and 2017?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To find inflation you must calculate the rate of change in the CPI.

To find the rate of change in the CPI you subtract the new CPI from the old CPI and divide by the old CPI, then multiply by $100\%$:

$\dfrac{CPI_2-CPI_1}{CPI_1}\times100\%$

Inflation between 2016 and 2017 in Ambrosia is calculated as:

$\begin{align}\text{Inflation rate}&=\dfrac{(220-200)}{200}\times100\%\\
\\
&=\dfrac{20}{200}\times100\%\\
\\
&=0.1 \times100\%\\
\\
&=10\%\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The price of hamster food and many other consumer goods increased in Hamsterville. As a result, the CPI increased from $80$ to $100$.

**What was the inflation rate in Hamsterville?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: To calculate the inflation rate, you find the difference between the new CPI and the old CPI, divide that by the old CPI, then multiply by $100\%$.

The formula to calculate the inflation rate is:

$\text{Inflation rate}=\dfrac{CPI_2-CPI_1}{CPI_1}\times 100\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
The GDP deflator in each of the past five years is given below:

Year | GDP deflator
-|-
2018 | $200$
2017 | $210$
2016 | $200$
2015 | $190$
2014 | $180$

**The rate of inflation was highest between which two years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The rate of inflation is the same every year
Between 2017 and 2018
Between 2016 and 2017
Between 2015 and 2016
Between 2014 and 2015

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the relationship between a worker's real wage and nominal wage when there is no inflation?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Real wage and nominal wage are the same
Real wage is lower than nominal wage
Real wage is higher than nominal wage
Both real and nominal wage increase, but in different amounts
Both real and nominal wage decrease, but in different amounts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is the best definition of a *real variable*?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: variables measured in current values
nominal values deflated by the price level
real variables deflated by the price level
nominal variables that are tracked over time
an increase in the price level

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a valid criticism of the consumer price index (CPI)?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The CPI overstates the burden of inflation on households because households can substitute cheaper goods instead of more expensive goods when prices increase.
The CPI understates the burden of inflation on households because households can substitute cheaper goods for more expensive goods when prices increase.
The CPI overstates the actual burden of inflation because people look around for cheaper goods, which is costly to do when prices increase.
The CPI understates the actual burden of inflation because the price changes are due to quality improvements.
The CPI overstates the actual burden of inflation because people can look around costlessly for cheaper goods when prices increase.
